stem: A county's population P, in thousands, is given by P = 240 - 3t, where t is the number of years since 2010. A neighboring county's population Q, in thousands, is given by Q = 180 - 2t. In what year will the difference between the two counties' populations be 40 thousand?
explanation: The difference between the populations is P - Q = (240 - 3t) - (180 - 2t) = 240 - 3t - 180 + 2t = 60 - t. We need this difference to equal 40: 60 - t = 40, so t = 20. Since t represents years since 2010, the year is 2010 + 20 = 2050.

distractorRationale.A: Student solves 240 - 180 = 60 and then incorrectly divides by the rate difference 3 - 2 = 1 to get 60 years, but then subtracts 40 to get 20 years, arriving at 2030. This reflects confusion about how the difference changes over time.
distractorRationale.B: Student sets up (240 - 3t) - (180 - 2t) = 40 but makes an algebraic error, getting 60 - 5t = 40, leading to 5t = 20, so t = 4. However, they then incorrectly calculate 2010 + 30 = 2040 by confusing intermediate values.
distractorRationale.C: Correct answer: The difference P - Q = 60 - t, setting equal to 40 gives t = 20, so year 2050.
distractorRationale.D: Student incorrectly interprets the problem as finding when P = 40 more than some baseline, setting 240 - 3t = 180 + 40 = 220, getting 3t = 20, so t ≈ 6.67. Then incorrectly multiplies by 9 (misunderstanding the rate) to get approximately 60, arriving at 2070.
